observations.recommendation:
To address the foregoing audit issues raised, it is recommended that:


The LGU consider rescinding the contract and subject the availment of services for waste disposal to bidding in compliance with RA 9184.
Requires the individual referred to as the Second Party in the MOA to issue Official Receipts for all the payments to already rendered services for the waste disposal of the LGU, otherwise all payments will be considered irregular, therefore subject to disallowance pursuant to COA Circular 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

The LGU establish its control mechanism in order that only actual weights of garbage disposal will be paid and control mechanism should include the prevention of connivance between the LGUs employees with the Urdaneta Waste Management.


This control mechanism would include an affidavit to be made by the drivers certifying the actual weights of garbage delivered. If possible the LGU can consider providing its own weighing scale.

We have recommended that Municipal Accountant see to it that supporting documents are complete before certifying the Disbursement Voucher and effecting payment. Compliance with the provisions of the law, rules and regulations pertaining to documentation of claims against government funds is strictly recommended.
observations.reason: For 2020, there is still observation on incomplete documentation and is reiterated in 2020 Annual Audit Report

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management immediately submit the supporting documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the aforementioned disbursement to establish the validity, propriety and legality of claims pursuant to Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the General Service Office and the Inventory Committee conduct
complete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s assets and prepare
the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment. Make
strategic actions/plan on how to conduct a complete physical inventory
of all properties of the municipality.

Moreover, the General Services Officer (GSO) to prepare corresponding
Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable property for the
identified unserviceable properties and initiate the process of disposing
the same in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and the rules provided
in COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify and evaluate the
nature and authenticity of the above transactions and prepare necessary
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the adjustments for these checks
to present an accurate and reliable balance of the Cash in Bank accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to determine the cause of
discrepancies of their respective reports and prepare the necessary
adjustments for a fair presentation of the financial statements. Ensure
that the accounts of the agency shall be kept in such detail as necessary
and observe consistency in keeping of accounts to safeguard against
inaccurate or misleading information pursuant to Section 111 of PD 1445
and other relevant rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant:
a. in close coordination with the General Services Officer to devise more
effective strategies to encourage the available/existing contractors/
suppliers to process the claims for refund/release of their retention
money to reduce the balance of the Guaranty/ Security Deposits
Payable for only those amounts which have not yet matured or are not
yet due for release; and
b. to evaluate the validity of the long outstanding payables and seek
authority, if needed from the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of
the long outstanding payables to Unappropriated Surplus of the
General Fund to reflect the correct balance of the liabilities accounts
as of year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the transfer of the
interest income totaling ₱87,733.17 to the General Fund as prescribed in
Section 112 of PD 1445 and Section 49, Chapter III, Volume I of the GAM
for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to record the procurement of supplies and
materials for operations into an appropriate inventory accounts and
credit the same for issuance to end-users;
b. Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to prepare and
maintain the Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively:
c. Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the
appropriate supplies expense accounts based on the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
a. The Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the liquidation
reports if not yet submitted and the check to the source agency for
refund of the unexpended balance. See to it that future fund transfers are
fully implemented within the time frame to avoid delay in liquidation; and
b. The Head of the Municipal Planning and Development Office
coordinate with the concerned department heads to enforce utilization
and implementation of those projects that are not implemented. Likewise,
see to it that their respective time frames and schedules are strictly
monitored and evaluated to avoid delays in the implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned
Departments:
a. to immediately present for audit the lacking documents to
substantiate afore-cited transactions and ensure that all documentary
requirements for emergency purchase are complied with before
certifying as to completeness of the supporting documents of the
disbursement vouchers;
b. to utilize the QRF only after issuance of a Local Sanggunian
Resolution to support such release including an itemized utilization
thereof pursuant to Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002;
c. that for succeeding transactions, secure authorization first from the
Sanggunian Bayan before any disbursements from a lump-sum
appropriation is made pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2010-014;
d. minimize if not eliminate the procurement of goods and services on a
reimbursement basis, unless the circumstances warrant its
application; and
e. ensure that all the procedures under negotiated procurement
(emergency cases) as required under Item 3 of GPPB Circular 01-2020
dated April 6, 2020 are undertaken and the required documents are
prepared to prevent abuses and corruption in the procurement
process.

For future similar transactions, the Municipal Accountant should ensure
that payments to suppliers/creditors be made based on complete
documents to support the claims pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and
guidelines under COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant in coordination with the Human Resource Development
Officer - Designate to submit the necessary documents to support the
grant of COVID-19 Hazard Pay and the one-time Special Risk Allowance
to officials, employees, job order workers and public health workers
during the ECQ period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to judiciously plan
on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM Funds including those
financial assistance received from other government agencies and to
accelerate their implementation in order to achieve the goal/target of the
Municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and
management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Reorganize the composition of Municipal Solid Waste Management
Board in accordance with Section 12 of RA 9003;
b. Modify the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan and ensure its
compliance with the National Solid Waste Management Framework
and coordinate with the Regional Office of the Environmental
Management Bureau and ensure that the same be approved by the
National Solid Waste Management Commission within the year;
c. Instruct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to assist all
barangays in the establishment of an individual or common Materials
Recovery Facility (MRF) and ensure the Municipality’s MRF is
existing and fully operational;
d. Address the issues already identified within the year for effective and
efficient Ecological Solid Waste Management in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Maintain GAD database and establish gender issues and concerns
through conduct of gender analysis, generation and review of sex-
disaggregated data and consultations with gender advocacies and
women clientele.
b. Require the GAD Focal Point Person to submit GAD Plan and Budget
to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval before its
integration to the Municipality’s annual appropriation;
c. Exert active efforts in accomplishing the targeted and planned Client-
focused and Agency-focused PPAs. Try not just to sustain its
compliance with minimum budget requirement on GAD programs,
projects and activities as provided by the law, but to increase
identification of gender-responsive PPAs to be covered by the annual
budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all the Department
Heads/employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the
CY 2019 AAR findings and recommendations, including that of prior
years, related/connected to their departments/offices and the information
gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan
and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the Audit Team.

Moreover, we recommended to follow strictly the above provisions of the
law on the submission of the AAPSI within 60 days from the receipt of the
COA Annual Audit report to give enough time for the Auditor to review,
monitor and evaluate the action taken by the management on the audit
recommendations.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to correct the presentation of the transfer of the ₱21,477,045.58 assets from the Trust Fund to the General Fund in accordance with the criteria set forth under Paragraph 17 of IPSAS 3.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to take up the correcting entries presented in the foregoing discussions and to ensure that reciprocal accounts reflect the same balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the (a) Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to obligate and record the Terminal Leave Benefits of a retired employee only upon the actual date of retirement and not when the employee signified his/her intention to retire, in order to conform with the expense recognition principle and to reflect the correct balances of the accounts, and (b) Municipal Accountant to record the journal entry to correct the balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to religiously follow the provisions of IPSAS 1 and the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs in recording financial transactions and that all taxes withheld which are to be remitted to the BIR must be recorded using the Due to BIR account in order to present fairly the accounts.

We recommended that Management instruct the General Services Officer to make sure that AREs issued will cover only those properties/equipment owned by the Municipality and to immediately cancel those AREs issued for donated/distributed properties as these are no longer owned by the Municipality pursuant to Section 42, Volume II of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly supervise the issuance of official receipts and ensure that these are properly and completely filled out by collecting officers and to immediately cancel and replace official receipts with errors in order to remove doubts on the propriety of all collections made by the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the GAD Focal Person to coordinate with all department heads responsible for the implementation of GAD PPAs to come up with strategic plans for the timely, efficient and effective implementation of these GAD PPAs in order to attain and deliver desirable benefits and outcomes to the beneficiaries.

We recommended that:
If the AO/ employee with unliquidated cash advance is still connected with the Municipality as of this date, Management shall instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the salary of the personnel pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and immediately require full liquidation of the subject long outstanding cash advance.
If the AO/ employee with unliquidated cash advance is no longer connected with the Municipality as of this date, Management shall:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Human Resource Management Officer to send demand letters to the former employees’ last known address and to enforce immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advance. They shall also coordinate with the Barangay Officials of the last known whereabouts of those personnel to ensure possibility of liquidation/ settlement of cash advance. They shall furnish the Office of the Auditor with copy of Demand letters duly received and the result of actions taken thereon.
In case the probability of collection is still nil and in case the former employee secures clearance from the agency, the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Human Resource Management Officer shall deduct from the last salary, Year – End Bonus, Terminal Leave Benefits and other benefits due him, the amount equivalent to the outstanding cash advance.

We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and property cards, respectively, and require them to reconcile these records regularly to have a verifiable, accurate and reliable PPE balances as presented in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize transactions in the books of accounts in accordance with the aforesaid accounting rules and regulations. The Municipal Accountant shall ensure that transactions are charged to the proper accounts in order to show the correct account balances in the financial statements. The following adjusting entry shall be made to recognize the Property, Plant and Equipment in the books of accounts:
Power Supply Systems		₱     320,365.92
Other Land Improvements		     1,370,109.38
Motor Vehicles			        199,200.00
Prior Period Adjustment			₱ 1,889,675.30

We recommended that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that all planned and budgeted programs and projects for the year under the 20% Development Fund be implemented as scheduled in the Annual Procurement Plan/ or within the Calendar Year pursuant to Item 5.0 of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and item 3.2.3 of DBM, DOF and DILG JMC No.1 dated November 4, 2020.  Optimal utilization of the fund and implementation of programs/ projects/ activities within the target completion date will maximize the benefits and opportunities that constituents/ beneficiaries could derive therefrom, hence, the municipality could contribute to lessen the socio-economic impact of the COVID 19 pandemic.
We also recommended that the status of implementation of the projects be included in the agenda of Municipal Development Council regular meeting to promote greater transparency and accountability in the LGUs' appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended Management thru the Municipal Accountant to transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the LDRRMF to Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code: 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books at the end of the year pursuant to item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No.2012-002 for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council within the next five (5) years.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the provisions of Manual on NGAS in safeguarding government funds by depositing intact all collections with the authorized government depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.  Moreover, regularly update the cashbooks by posting the collections on a daily basis and serve as monitoring guide on how much collections were due for deposit.

We recommended Management to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and limit granting of cash advance to allowable transactions stated therein to avoid exposure to susceptibility risks associated with it, exposing these municipal funds to theft, misuse, misapplication and misappropriation.

We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere to the required number of prepared, sent and obtained RFQs/ RFPs      for Small Value Procurement as required under Item No. V – D (No. 8 [b]) of Annex “H” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in order to promote economy and efficiency in the procurement process.

We recommended Management to adhere to the provisions of Section 61 of PD No. 1445 on authorization of government expenditures particularly on gasoline expenses and to the internal controls on fuel consumption of government motor vehicles to prevent the incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, excessive and unconscionable gasoline expenditures which may be disallowed in audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-003.
We also recommended Management to instruct the personnel in-charge of issuing gas slip to have a complete list of all municipal motor vehicles and with the list of corresponding maximum fuel tank capacity of each vehicle as a guide/reference in approving the number of liters in the gas slip before an authorized driver can procure gasoline.  There shall be proper monitoring of completeness of details in the authorized gas slip and driver’s trip ticket and the correctness of supplier’s invoice/ billing statement before payment for appropriate and/ or accurate gasoline expenses.

We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly review that all supporting documents are complete before certifying the Disbursement Vouchers and effecting payment. Compliance with the provisions of the law, rules and regulations pertaining to documentation of claims against government funds is strictly recommended.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal GSO and Inventory Committee to:
a. conduct complete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s assets and prepare the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment. Make strategic actions/plan on how to conduct a complete physical inventory of all properties of the municipality.
b. prepare corresponding Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable property for the identified unserviceable properties and initiate the process of disposing the same in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and the rules provided in COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee started the physical count of the properties of the agency, but was not able to reconcile the discrepancies. The Inventory Committee failed to finish the physical count of the properties of the agency. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO started to evaluate the causes of the discrepancies to ascertain the fairness and reliability of the PPE balances in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The Inventory committee failed to finish the physical count. The Municipal Accountant and the GSO started to evaluate the causes of the discrepancies. The discrepancies of the PPE balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to – a. reconcile the difference between the balances of Real Property Tax (RPT) and Special Education Tax (SET) Receivable; b. conduct a regular reconciliation of records with the Municipal Treasurer updating/ starting on the reconciling their records with the Municipal Assessor’s records. c. maintain the subsidiary ledgers for the RPT and SET Receivables in order to validate the accounts presented in the financial statements and to ensure accuracy and reliability of the balances reported monthly; and d. review all entries reflected in the journals to ensure that all recorded adjustments are correct and complete to avoid the incurrence of deficiencies among records. Effect the necessary adjustment/s, if any.
B. Tax delinquencies reported by the Treasurer’s Office as of December 31, 2019 showed an ending balance of ₱1,240,408.10, including penalties, with an increase of ₱688,209.56 from its previous amount of ₱552,198.54, contrary to Sections 254, 258 and 269 of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, thereby showing also the weakness on enforcing collections and depriving the Municipality of additional funds which could have been used to implement more beneficial projects/programs for its constituents. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer – a. and other concerned officials to implement more effective, efficient and aggressive tax campaign and develop additional strategies to improve tax revenue generation and to intensify collection of Real Property Taxes not only current but delinquent taxes in order to generate more income; and b. to conduct verification on the outstanding balances to determine the accuracy of the receivables and its collectability.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer commented that they have started updating the set-up of RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the period and adjustment of the accounts upon collection. The Treasurer had started updating/ reconciling their records.
observations.reason: The Treasurer started updating their records. Some verification on outstanding balances and additional tax campaign actions to intensify collection were recommended but not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the perpetual inventory method in the inventory and issuance of materials as provided under the IPSAS 12 and discontinue the practice of recording the purchases of supplies and materials as outright expenses upon purchase; maintain the subsidiary ledger cards for each item of supplies; b. Municipal General Services Officer to maintain Stock Cards (SC) and Property Cards for the various classes of supplies; c. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventories and prepare the Report of Physical Count of Inventories to ascertain the actual existence of said assets and for the systematic reconciliation of records with the Accounting and GSO. Likewise, furnish the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for proper evaluation thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and GSO had started the preparation of subsidiary ledger cards and stock cards. The Municipal Accountant will draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the supplies expense accounts based on the SSMI. The Inventory Committee started the physical inventory and prepared reports.
observations.reason: Some inventory records were started but not fully completed; physical count and reconciliation not finished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Chairman of the Committee on Disposal to initiate the immediate disposal of those properties in accordance with afore-cited provisions in order not to expose those properties to elements that cause further deterioration or salvaged value and to generate additional income thereof; and b. GSO-Designate to: (i) organize, secure and ensure that the unserviceable PPEs are stored properly and in the orderly manner as much as possible to facilitate the easy identification of the assets item per item and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the proper determination of their costs; and (ii) revise the IIRUP by presenting the unserviceable PPE items by class and not on a per department basis.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had asked the help of the GSO to contact those suppliers/contractors to get their claims for the release/refund of their retention money, some of the claims were already released to the suppliers/contractors.
observations.reason: Some unserviceable properties are stored properly, while others were not and costing/identification was not yet made by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Reorganize the composition of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in accordance with Section 12 of RA 9003; b. Modify the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan and ensure its compliance with the National Solid Waste Management Framework and coordinate with the Regional Office of the Environmental Management Bureau and ensure that the same be approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission within the year; c. Instruct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to assist all barangays in the establishment of an individual or common Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) and ensure the Municipality’s MRF is existing and fully operational; d. Address the issues already identified within the year for effective and efficient Ecological Solid Waste Management in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The officer concerned commented that the composition of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board was already changed, waiting for the approval of Administrative Order from the Office of the Municipal Mayor. Further, he commented that due to pandemic, the MRFs in some of the barangays were still not yet established due to some financial issues of the municipality.
observations.reason: Some barangay MRFs were established and the composition changed but central MRF not yet operational and 10-Year Plan not yet modified/approved; pandemic and financial issues delayed establishment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer – (a) to submit detailed explanation/justification citing the reasons why the remaining balance of ₱711,090.00 was still carried in the books despite the completion of the six (6) PPAs with total cost of ₱10,632,130.00 were either partially completed and/or unimplemented as of year-end despite the availability of funds; and (b) to coordinate with the responsible departments to reduce unnecessary delays in project implementation and strictly effect the necessary processes to expedite the completion of the not-yet started and on-going projects to allow the early enjoyment of benefits by the general public and the full utilization of the funds received from National Government Agencies (NGAs); and b. to coordinate with the source agencies and return the unutilized funds pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and DOF-DBM-COA Permanent Committee Joint Circular No. 4-2012 to avoid possible fund misuse.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant said that the unutilized portion of the completed projects were already remitted to the source agencies, while the unimplemented amount for the several projects have started the implementation at the start of CY 2021.
observations.reason: Some funds were returned; implementation of other projects delayed due to Taal volcano eruption and pandemic; some projects started in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM Funds including those financial assistance received from other government agencies and to accelerate their implementation in order to achieve the goal/target of the Municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and management. b. require the MDRRMO to include in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be charged against the unexpended appropriation of previous years or those funds under the Trust Liability-DRRM and submit the list of PPAs funded or to be funded from the unexpended prior years DRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction: The management commented that their implementation of some PPAs were postponed/ delayed due to pandemic.
observations.reason: Implementation delayed due to pandemic; some PPAs postponed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the chairperson of the Municipal Government, direct the Municipal Human Resources Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant to institute effective controls and monitoring of the payrolls deductions to ensure that net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum amount of ₱5,000.00 as provided under the Annual GAA.
observations.managementAction: The HR and the Implementing officers had instituted effective controls and monitoring of the employees’ payrolls particularly the deductions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) Ensure the Municipal Council to prepare a separate Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC) and Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC) and maximize the utilization of the allocated LCPC Fund to identified programs, projects and activities (PPAs) provided for in the afore-mentioned Circular to ensure full strengthening and implementation of the LCPC; and b) require the Municipal Social Welfare Officer, being the focal person, to ensure that the identified PPAs geared for promotion of rights and welfare of children are carried out effectively and efficiently and continuously monitor the implementation of children’s programs and projects in the locality.
observations.managementAction: They have prepared a Plan for Local Development (LDPC) and Plan for Local Children Investment Plan for Children and submitted. The Municipal Social Welfare Officer to ensure that the identified PPAs are implemented. The management commented that they will comply.
observations.reason: Not all PPAs were implemented; some lapses remained.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct:

(a) The Accounting Department and GSO to exert extra efforts to reconcile the un-reconciled balances of the  accounts and after which the Accounting Department prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries, if appropriate, in order to come up with the correct balances; and

(b) The GSO to maintain and put property tags of all properties of the municipality in order to prevent the risk of loss/theft.

Management submitted a revised RCPPE and upon review, the RPCPPE reconciled with the accounting records.

Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality stop the practice of recording directly as expense the purchases of inventory items pursuant to the NGAS Manual for LGUs. It is further recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the erroneous recording in order to present fairly the balance of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: All purchases have already been coursed through the inventory account except for purchases done for immediate use.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

Issue Memorandum to the Municipal Treasurer for not adhering to the rules and regulation governing intact deposit of all collections and safekeeping of municipal funds against risk of possible loss, misapplication and/or unauthorized use pursuant to Section 32 of Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 101 of PD 1445 respectively.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit Daily Collection and Deposit Report to ensure that all collections are deposited intact and no yesterday collections (except on holidays and fortuitous events) remains in the Cash in Treasury for monitoring compliance of the Municipal Treasurer to this Audit Observation Memorandum.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that:

The Municipal Treasurer require his collecting agents to prepare and submit daily the Report of Collections and Deposits which will be the basis for recording in the Cashbook and General Ledger. 

The Cashbooks and General Ledger to be updated on a daily basis and reconcile at least at month-end or as the need arises to ensure accuracy and reliability of its presentation in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to stop from the practice of splitting of contracts.  Instead strictly abide to the provisions of RA 9184 in all procurement activities to promote good governance and adhere to the principle of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy, to the advantage of the LGU.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to strictly observe pursuant to the above cited regulations to prevent reduction of appropriation for the current year and avoid the likelihood of possible budget overdraft. Thru an ordinance, supplement the current year appropriation by the same amount of the prior year expenses charged to current operation, to prevent disallowances of the above cited expenditures.

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant, to strictly monitor flow of Disbursement Vouchers in preventing late submission and overlook in recording. 


Furthermore, the Disbursing Officer, to report regularly to the Municipal Accountant of list of expenditures due for payment for accrual purposes, in order to reflect it fairly in the financial statements, in compliance to IPSAS 1, requiring faithful representation in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor/Local Chief Executive the following:

Ensure strict compliance with the provisions of Section 21 of RA No. 10121 in the appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF.  

Instruct the Accountant to transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the LDRRMF to Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code: 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books at the end of the year pursuant to item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No.2012-002 for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council within the next five (5) years.
observations.managementAction: The amount of the balance of the LDRRMF was transferred to Trust Fund during the year.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:


The accountable officers adhere strictly to the above cited provisions to avoid the possible fraudulent use of accountable forms and ensure that all accountable forms in the custody of the accountable officers are kept diligently; and 

The Municipal Mayor direct the accountable officer to locate the missing Plates and Stickers. In the event that these can no longer be located/presented, a request for relief from accountability for the loss of accountable forms shall be made.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant to record inventory using the prescribed perpetual method of accounting on all purchased supplies and use inventory tags or stock cards for monitoring. 

Likewise, conduct regular reconciliation of the ledger and the stock cards to ensure that all purchases and issuances are properly accounted for.  With the above recommendation takes place, the municipality have the assurance that inventories are safeguarded against risk of misapplication or misuse and ensure the reliability and accuracy of the accounts presented in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct:
(a) The Accounting Department and GSO to exert extra efforts to reconcile the un-reconciled balances of the  accounts and after which the Accounting Department prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries, if appropriate, in order to come up with the correct balances; and

We recommended that the municipality through the Accounting Department provide depreciation for all depreciable assets pursuant to paragraph 43 and 71 of IPSAS 17 and record the necessary correcting entries. It is further recommended that said department reconcile the discrepancy between the FS/Post-closing Trial Balance and Schedule of Depreciation and thereafter make the necessary adjusting journal entries if applicable.

We recommended that the municipality stop the practice of recording directly as expense the purchases of inventory items pursuant to the NGAS Manual for LGUs. It is further recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the erroneous recording in order to present fairly the balance of the accounts.

We recommended that the municipality through the Accounting Department submit the supporting schedule showing the breakdown of the amount reported as Changes in Accounting Policy in order to determine whether such changes are in accordance with the requirements set forth by IPSAS. Also, disclose these changes in the Notes to FS for a better understanding of the users of financial information.

We recommended that the Accounting Department analyze and review the subsidiary ledger(s) for other deferred credits account to ensure that only valid obligations falling within that category are recorded in the account. It is further recommended that the correcting journal entries be prepared in order to present fairly the financial statements.

We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer adhere to the Omnibus Rules on Leave and COA Circular 2012-001 in the processing of Terminal Leave Benefits and submit/rectify immediately the lacking/deficient documents. It is further recommended that the accountant review the correctness and completeness of all supporting documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 before subjecting disbursement vouchers for payment to avoid suspensions or disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer review and scrutinize the applications for leave of the municipal officials and employees to ensure that these are filed, processed and approved in accordance with the Omnibus Rules on Leave.

We recommended that the Property inspector determine first the nature of the items of waste material and subsequently effect the proper disposal pursuant to Section 58 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS). It is further recommended that strict internal control must be effected in the disposal of waste materials in order not to deprive the municipality of additional funds if these items are sold.

We recommended that the municipality strictly comply with the provisions of Section 52 of the GAA 2019 by screening the loan application of its employees so that the monthly net take home pay will not be reduced to an amount below ₱5,000.00 after all the authorized deductions were reflected in the general payroll. Also, restructure the deductions particularly debt servicing of the concerned employees to conform to the required amount of net take home pay.
We recommended that Management stop the practice of allowing employees to claim for registration and similar fees from the government on meetings not covered by NBC 563 dated April 26, 2016.

We recommended that the Accounting Department and the Bids and Awards Committee () make sure that the quantity and specifications stated in the PR, RFQ and Inspection and Acceptance Report are correct and the same pursuant to the revised IRR of RA 9184. It is further recommended that the amount paid for the purchase of goods and services is correct and matches with the RFQ and Purchase Order/Job Order.

We recommended that the municipality through the Accounting Department and Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office:
exhaust all possible means to enforce payment on the amount of ₱311,695.00 from the persons accountable/liable such as issuing demand letters and regular visitation of the persons liable; and
if collection proves futile, initiate efforts to request for write-off pursuant to the Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances, and Fund Transfers set forth under COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommended that the municipality strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely submission of the said reports to the COA Audit Team and update its GAD Code to improve the municipality’s policies to better implement its GAD Mandate.

We recommended that the liable personnel and officials immediately settle the ₱1,027,000.00 and ₱169,000.00 disallowance that is not under appeal in accordance with the rules and regulations on the settlement of accounts.

We have reiterated the prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor shall:
Create an Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count and prepare the RPCPPE necessary for control and monitoring of PPEs. Incorporate in the RCPPE the whereabouts, serviceability and physical condition, as basis for impairment. In effect, the accuracy, validity, existence and actual condition could be ascertained and render the account not doubtful.
Require the Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of Supply Officer, and the Municipal Accountant to immediately start and regularly maintain their respective property and ledger cards per category of property, plant and equipment to record deliveries of properties in the ensuing year.  Incorporate in their property and ledgers cards the whereabouts, serviceability and physical condition as stated in the RCPPE for reference and verification purposes and
Require the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile the book records with the inventory report as internal control over the PPEs of the municipality.

We have reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendation that the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly complied.
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network.
Require the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.
Create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System and
Compliance with Item IN9 of IPSAS No. 1 is strictly recommended.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit Daily Collection and Deposit Report to ensure that all collections are deposited intact and no yesterday collections (except on holidays and fortuitous events) remains in the Cash in Treasury for monitoring compliance of the Municipal Treasurer to this Audit Observation Memorandum.

We have recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report and approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We have recommended  that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to stop from the practice of splitting of contracts.  Instead strictly abide to the provisions of RA 9184 in all procurement activities to promote good governance and adhere to the principle of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy, to the advantage of the LGU.

Requires the individual referred to as the Second Party in the MOA to issue Official Receipts for all the payments to already rendered services for the waste disposal of the LGU, otherwise all payments will be considered irregular, therefore subject to disallowance pursuant to COA Circular 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

This control mechanism would include an affidavit to be made by the drivers certifying the actual weights of garbage delivered. If possible the LGU can consider providing its own weighing scale.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor/Local Chief Executive the following:

Instruct the Accountant to transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the LDRRMF to Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code: 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books at the end of the year pursuant to item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No.2012-002 for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council within the next five (5) years.

We have recommended that Municipal Accountant see to it that supporting documents are complete before certifying the Disbursement Voucher and effecting payment. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. instruct the Municipal GSO together with the Inventory Committee to conduct a
complete physical count of the properties, reconcile the results thereof with
accounting records and submit the same to the Audit Team to ascertain the
existence, validity and correctness of the Property, Plant and Equipment as well as

financial statements.

The Municipal GSO commented that he will coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for
the reconciliation of records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office, to:
(i) conduct physical inventory of all existing local road networks in the City and
prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network
to be reconciled with the accounting records; and (ii) furnish a copy of such

basis for reconciliation with the accounting records;

Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the prescribed format as
provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015;
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer
and the Municipal Engineer to (i) be mindful of their mandated duties and
responsibilities outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-008 to ensure that the
components of Road Network System of the Municipality of San Nicolas are
properly identified, valued and accounted for in the books and completely
presented in the financial statements and (ii) prioritize the immediate identification
of the local road networks for CY 2016 and below to reflect the complete balance of
Road Network account in the financial statements; and ensure good working
coordination among LGU offices to strictly follow and implement the accounting
and reporting guidelines on the Local Road Network System.
Management’s Comment:
Management has not yet provided any reply to date.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

 Municipal Accountant to –
a. reconcile the difference between the balances of Real Property Tax (RPT)
Receivable and Special Education Tax (SET) Receivable;
b. conduct a regular reconciliation of records with the Municipal Treasurer
starting on the set-up of RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the
period up to the consistent adjustment of these accounts upon collection;
c. maintain the necessary subsidiary ledgers for the RPT and SET Receivables in
order to validate the balances of the accounts presented in the financial
statements and to ensure accuracy and reliability of the balances reported
monthly; and
d. review all entries reflected in the journals to ensure that all recorded
adjustments are correct and complete to avoid the incurrence of deficiencies
among records. Effect the necessary adjustment/s, if any.
 Municipal Treasurer –
a. and other concerned officials to implement more effective, efficient and
aggressive synergized tax campaign and develop additional strategies to
improve tax revenue generation and to intensify collection of Real Property
Taxes not only current but also the delinquent taxes in order to generate more
income; and
b. to conduct verification on those outstanding balances to determine the
accuracy of the receivables and its collectability.

The Municipal Treasurer thru her letter reply dated September 18, 2020 commented that
the discrepancies found on the Real Property Tax Delinquencies were the result of the
updating/reconciliation conducted by her office with the records of the Municipal Assessor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the perpetual inventory method in
the recording of inventory and issuance of supplies and materials as
provided under the IPSAS 12 and discontinue the practice of treating the

Cards for the various classes of supplies.

Management has not yet provided any reply to date.

the Accounting and GSO. Likewise, furnish the Auditor concerned not later than

Management’s Comment:
Management has not yet provided any reply to date.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist and other concerned officials
properly monitor the LGU’s dispersal program and submit the report on the
dispersal of the breeding stocks indicating the name and complete address of the
recipients, description, quantity, unit cost and total amount of the breeding animals

Treasurer and the Municipal Agriculturist to exert effort to enforce and collect the

The Municipal Agriculturist commented that she already prepared a monitoring report on

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Chairman of the Committee on Disposal to initiate the immediate disposal of
those properties in accordance with afore-cited provisions in order not to
expose those properties to elements that cause further deterioration or loss of
salvaged value and to generate additional income thereof;
b. GSO-Designate to: (i) organize, secure and ensure that the unserviceable PPEs

basis.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
set-up and maintain Special accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of
operating performance and viability of the Municipality’s economic enterprises and
20% Development Fund, and to serve as a decision making tool of the agency. Also,
record all transactions pertaining to these economic enterprises to the
corresponding subsidiary ledgers.

The Municipal Accountant commented that she is still waiting for the separate budget of
the special accounts before separating the same from the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor reorganize the composition of
Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in accordance with Section 12 of RA
9003.

of PSWMB-TWG, ensure its consistencies with the National Solid Waste
Management Framework and properly coordinate with the Regional Office
of the Environmental Management Bureau its submission and ensure that the
same be approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission
within the year;
b. assist all barangays in the establishment of an individual or common
Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) and ensure that the Municipality’s MRF is

and fully implement the said law within its jurisdiction. Likewise, address the
issues already identified within the year for effective and efficient Ecological
Solid Waste Management in the Municipality.

Management has not yet provided any reply to date.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. to submit detailed explanation/justification citing the reasons why the
remaining balance of ₱711,090.00 was still carried in the books despite the

funds received from National Government Agencies (NGAs);

enumerated on 1 to 10 are already due for refund on September 2020. While savings from

respectively, for other related projects while the Share on Building Permit totaling ₱727.11
has been transferred to Trust Liability.

(MDRRMO) to judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM
Funds and to accelerate their implementation in order to achieve the goal/target
of the Municipal regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and
management; and

against the unexpended appropriation of previous years or those funds under
the Trust Liability-DRRM and submit the list of PPAs funded or to be funded

Management has not yet provided any reply to date.

Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant to institute effective controls

provided under the Annual GAA.

Management has not yet provided any reply to date.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Chairperson of the Executive
Committee and the Technical Working Group:
a. Ensure the proper mainstreaming of GAD related activities in all offices/
departments of the Municipality;
b. Submit the GAD Plan and Budget to the Provincial Planning and Development
Office (PPDO) to ensure the alignment of LGU GAD PPAs to the priorities of the
province on or before the deadline required by the Joint Memorandum Circular;
c. Secure first the Certificate of Endorsement of the Annual Gender and
Development (GAD) Plan and Budget (GPB) from the DILG Provincial Office
before incorporating to the Municipality’s Annual Budget to properly ascertain the
relevance of the gender issues identified and gender-responsiveness of the PPAs
indicated in the GPB;
d. Exert active efforts in accomplishing the targeted and planned Client-Focused
and Organization-Focused PPAs. Endeavor not just to sustain its compliance
with the minimum budget requirement on GAD programs, projects and activities
as provided for by the law, but to increase identification of gender-responsive
PPAs to be covered by the annual budget until such time that all major programs
of the Municipality are attributed to GAD as envisioned under the PCW-NEDA-
DBM Joint Circular;
e. Strictly monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs as required under the Joint
Circular and conduct regular coordination with the implementing offices to
ensure that programs are undertaken within the target period; and

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:

Create an Inventory   Committee to conduct annual physical count and prepare the RPCPPE necessary for control and monitoring of PPEs.  In effect, the accuracy, validity, existence and actual condition could be ascertained and render the account not doubtful.

The Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of Supply Officer, and the Municipal Accountant start maintaining their respective property and ledger cards per category of property, plant and equipment to record deliveries of properties in the ensuing year.

observations.reason: Management conducted inventory, however, form made intended for turnover purposes, including therein that PPEs are classified as serviceable and unserviceable. observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer had prepared inventory of road networks, however, the cost provided in the inventory did not reconcile to the Accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and limit granting of cash advance to allowable transactions stated therein to avoid any risk of possible misapplication or misused of local government funds.

We have recommended the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the provisions of MNGAS in safeguarding government funds by depositing intact all collections with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.  Moreover, regularly update the cashbooks by posting the collections on a daily basis, and serve as monitoring guide on how much collections were due for deposit.
We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive reprimand the Municipal Treasurer for non-compliance.

We have recommended that concern officials to strictly comply with the provisions concerning budget as stated.  Furthermore, we recommend the Budget Officer to exercise fiscal restraint in the management of the municipality’s resources by not allowing expenditures which are beyond the approved budget to avoid overdraft. Likewise, establish and maintain Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations to be used as a monitoring tool in the utilization of set budgets to stop the incurrence of overdrafts.

In order to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension for the aforementioned transactions which could be disallowed in audit if not settled by the persons responsible within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006, we have recommended that the management immediately submit the documents required under item 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other documents deemed necessary to establish the validity of disbursement for the purchase of lot/ land.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer  discontinue the practice of charging prior year’s expenditure to current year appropriation and that proper matching of expenses against appropriation should always be observed pursuant to the above-cited regulations.

We have reiterated the prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record transactions in the books of accounts in accordance with accounting rules and regulations. The Municipal Accountant should ensure that transactions are charged to the proper accounts in order to show the correct account balances in the financial statements.
Moreover, the following entry shall be made to recognize the Property, Plant and Equipment in the books.
Office Equipment		P 469,884.00
Furniture and Fixture	    585,132.00
Computer Software		      39,950.00
Government Equity		P 1,094,966.00
Lastly, compliance with IPSAS IN9 is strictly recommended.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant to record inventory using the prescribed perpetual method of accounting on all purchased supplies and use inventory tags or stock cards for monitoring. Likewise, conduct regular reconciliation of the ledger and the stock cards to ensure that all purchases and issuances are properly accounted for.  With the above recommendation takes place, the municipality have the assurance that inventories are safeguarded against risk of misapplication or misuse and ensure the reliability and accuracy of the accounts presented in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) Committee to prepare its succeeding budget based on the projects, programs and activities which are allowed and listed in Item 4.0 of the Joint Circular No. 01 series of 2017 of DEPED, DBM and DILG. Likewise, the municipal government torefrain from the practice of charging non-related expenses to SEF which is considered irregular expenditure and could be the basis for the audit team to issue Notice of Suspension/ Disallowance.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to separate incompatible duties particularly cash collection and cash disbursing functions in compliance with DOF-BLGF Local Treasury Operations Manual - Annex 87 Internal Control Concepts, Policies and Elements to prevent risks of committing errors and irregularities, merging of cash accountabilities, lessen difficulties of real time monitoring, prevent cover-up of cash shortages, and prevent fraud & misapplication of municipal funds.

We have recommended that the management:
Conduct analysis of the Loans Receivable – Account and submit correct Report of The Status of Fund Transfers to Non-Governmental Organizations/ People’s Organizations required under COA Memorandum No. 2017-010 with supporting documents
Revisit the Loan Agreement/ Memorandum of Agreement and issue demand letters to its debtors
For receivables with no available document but are not yet considered dormant, a sworn certification by the Accountant shall be submitted indicating, among others, the reasons for the loss of documents, and the person/s responsible thereof
Request for write-off of dormant Receivable Accounts, if any, pursuant to the guidelines set under COA Circular  No. 2016-005
Institute administrative charges to erring officials and employees, if warranted for neglect of duty

We have recommended that the management submit the aforementioned supporting documents establishing the impairment of Receivables-Disallowances/ Charges and Other Receivables amounting to P519,488.52 and P6,601,115.13, respectively in pursuance of IPSAS 29 and COA Circulars 2016-005 and COA Memorandum No. 2017-010.  Otherwise, reversal of impairment loss shall be effected.

We have recommended that the officials concerned shall strictly adhere to the prescribed period relative to the timely submission of monthly reports so that the Audit Team could also perform the timely review and evaluation of the agency’s financial transactions and communicate the audit results to the management for decision making.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list of delinquent taxpayers and posting of notice of delinquency in the municipal hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each Barangays.  Moreover, send notice to individual delinquent taxpayers reminding them of their respective real property tax dues and state therein the possible legal consequences and remedies of the government if case of failure to settle the tax obligation.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General
Service Officer to perform complete physical count of PPE and prepare
accordingly the RPCPPE to be submitted to the Accounting Office for comparison
and reconciliation. We also recommended that both departments must coordinate
with each other to reconcile the noted difference per PPE account. Finally, the
Accounting Office must draw the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the
reconciled balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
adopt accrual accounting in recording transactions relative to real property tax.
Consequently, she must set up RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the
year and record transactions affecting it so as to reflect their true balances at the
end of the year.

Foregoing considered, we recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the
Municipal Accountant to record biological assets existing as of yearend but were
acquired prior to 2017 and the receivables representing the Municipality’s claims
on the proceeds of sales. She must also adopt the correct accounting policy of
recording biological assets and the related income in accordance with PPSAS 27
and draw the adjusting entries to reflect their true balances as of yearend.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Agriculturist to provide information such as the quantity, age and weight of
existing animals which will serve as reference for the costing, valuation and
recording of the same by the Municipal Accountant.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist, to enforce the
collection of the proceeds from the sale of those animals still belong to the
agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
make sure that the required reconciliation are disclosed in the Notes to Financial
Statements in compliance with PPSAS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Social Welfare
and Development Officer to provide justification in their selection of beneficiaries
and to consider the order of priority prescribed under DSWD Administrative Order
No. 15 series of 2010 in selecting the same.

Foregoing considered, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
HRMO and the Municipal Accountant to provide justification for the difference
noted between the government dues withheld and actually remitted. The
Municipal Treasurer must pay the amount as withheld whereas the Municipal
Accountant must draw the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the true
transactions.

Hence, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Service
Department to duly apply for request for disposal of their unserviceable property
and to submit completely all the required documents therefor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Ensure that the 20% DF is optimally utilized in order to deliver the benefits
of desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes for the
Municipality and to the intended beneficiaries of each project; and
b. Direct the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to implement fully the identified
development projects under the 20% DF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMO to ensure that
the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness for the current
year as well as those for the utilization of the previous years’ LDRRMF transferred
to the Trust Fund are presented in the LDRRMFIP in detail as to the specific
projects and activities to be funded, as required under Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular
No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
revert the P183,723.10 balance of the CY 2013 Trust Liabilities in the Trust Fund to
the General Fund.

Foregoing considered, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
GAD Focal Person to collect and keep sex disaggregated data and to perform
gender analysis so as to concretely define its gender issues, to accordingly
design programs and activities that could most address them and, ultimately, to
ensure that its fund is utilized to the purpose it is intended. Similarly, the
Municipality must include gender analysis in its capacity development.
