Initiate the filing of appropriate Administrative Proceedings for Gross Neglect of Duty to those who failed to render account of their accountabilities after demand or Simple Neglect of Duty to those who failed to liquidate their cash advances in full within the prescribed periods pursuant to CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12, s. 2012

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly bank reconciliation statement of the Cash in Bank account of the municipality and submit a copy thereof to the auditor within the required period for review.

We have recommended that that the municipal treasurer:
Require all Revenue Collecting Personnel to remit their collections at the close of each business day pursuant to provisions of Section 29 of the Manual of NGAS, Volume I; and
Ensure that no additional accountable forms would be issued unless the collectors have fully remitted their previous collections

We have recommended that Management require the cash accountable officers to issue ORs in numerical sequence to ease the monitoring of said accountable form and avoid possible shortage and misuse of collections.

We have recommended that the Petty Cash Custodian, the Officials and Employees concerned should strictly adhere the Fifteen Thousand Pesos limitation for each Petty Cash Voucher as required by COA rules and regulations.
We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their book balances regularly in accordance with the provisions of Section 181 of GAAM Volume 1.

We have recommended that Management discontinue the payment of RATA to Sangguniang Bayan members who failed to attend the weekly sessions and require the SB members to accomplish application for leave if applicable, for recording in leave cards pursuant to Section 52 of RA 7160 and item 7.5 of Local Budget Circular No. 103.
We have recommended that management should refrain from donating government funds to religious and private organizations in compliance with the provisions of Section 335 of the Local Government Code.
We have recommended that the accountant should ensure that travelling expenses are made within the prescribed limits in accordance with EO 298.

We have recommended that the municipality use the appropriate accounts prescribed by COA Circular No. 2006-001 in under the cash accounts so that the susceptibility of the fund to misapplication and misuse may be reduced.
We have recommended that the municipality prepare the necessary requirements before consummating a procurement.  The provisions of New Government Accounting Standards should be strictly adhered to.

We have recommended that management strictly adhere to the rules and regulations in the procurement of goods and services such that competitive public bidding be adopted in all its procurement activities The Bids and Awards Committee, who has the responsibility for ensuring that the procuring entity abides by the standards set forth by RA 9184 and its IRR, should strictly observe the provisions on the use of alternative mode of procurement before resorting thereto to ensure that the most advantageous prices for the government are obtained.

We have recommended management to furnish the Auditors copies of contracts together with all the supporting documents to the COA within five (5) working days from execution thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 87-278 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 as reiterated in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive should require the Bids and Award Committee together with the BAC Secretariat and the Technical Working Group Members to strictly observe and adhere to all the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184 on all their procurement transactions.

Also, they should invite in writing, at least three observers from Commission on Audit, from duly recognized private group, and from Non-government Organization to enhance transparency of the process.
We have also recommended that copies of all contracts, preparatory requirements, and their supporting documents should be submitted to our office within five (5) working days upon completion of the contract in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We have recommended that the municipality enforce through the Bids and Awards Committee the procurement of commonly used supplies and equipment from the PS-DBM in compliance with the above-cited DBM Circular and Administrative Order.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to enjoined all employees of the municipality to comply with the requirements set forth under the General Appropriations Act and adopt control measures to ensure that the employees monthly net take home pay not be lower than required minimum take home pay after deducting mandatory contributions and other obligations.

We have reiterated that the management observed that every time that there is unexpended balance of the LDRRMF as of yearend, it should be transferred to Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code: 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books.

We have recommended that the municipality require the concerned offices to implement all activities included in their GPB for the benefit of intended beneficiaries.

We have further recommended the GFPS to scrutinize the amounts being charged to GAD to preserve the validity of accomplishment.

We again recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require  the  Inventory
Committee  to  conduct  the  physical  count  of  all  Property,  Plant  and
Equipment at least once a year to ensure its existence, prepare  the  Report
on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment  (RPCPPE)  with  complete
information and submit to the Accounting Office for reconciliation.


      We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Accountant to effect the necessary adjustment in the books  of  account  the
transfer of Construction in Progress-Buildings and  Structures  to  Building
account with  the  corresponding  Accumulated  Depreciation.  Likewise,  the
Municipal Accountant should  strictly  observe  COA  Circular  No.  2015-008
regarding transfer of prior years’ Public Infrastructures.


      We advised that the General  Services  Officer  determine  the  actual
condition of unserviceable properties so  that  proper  disposition  can  be
made which  may  be  thru  destruction  or  sale  in  public  auction  after
inspection and appraisal of COA representative.  For those  equipment  which
were disposed, submit the documents authorizing the disposition  of  these
properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:


     a) Municipal Accountant to recognize in the LGU books  of accounts the
        assessed value of the 17 parcels of land;


     b) Budget Officer to request from the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate
        funds for the titling of land owned by the Municipality;


     c) General Services Officer to conduct  site  inspection  and  prepare
        reports on the actual usage of each property; and


     d) Municipal Assessor to retrieve required information  and  documents
        necessary for the titling and refer the  matter  to  the  concerned
        agencies to facilitate the processing of title.

Management’s Comment

      In reply to the AOM issued by the team, the Municipal Assessor  stated
that he will coordinate with the concerned agency to facilitate the  titling
of government properties in  their  Municipality  and  will  report  to  the
Municipal  Mayor  the  result  of  information  gathered  from  the   agency
concerned.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  concerned
municipal  officials  to  exert  effort  to  collect  those   long   overdue
receivables.  If  effort  proved  futile,  the  Municipality  can  have  the
accounts written off, upon prior approval of the Commission on Audit.

Management’s Comment

      The Municipal Accountant will gather all the documents regarding these
receivables and will request for write off of dormant accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify and  evaluate  the
nature and authenticity of the above transactions and prepare the  necessary
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the adjustments for  these  checks  to
present an accurate and reliable balance of the Cash in Bank accounts.


     The Municipal Accountant committed to adjust the stale checks  in  the
ensuing year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  direct  the  concerned  local
officials that all projects charged against 20 percent Development  Fund  be
implemented in accordance with the provisions cited in DILG  and  DBM  Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1. Refrain from charging salaries and wages  of
job orders to avoid disallowance in audit.

Management’s Comment

      The Municipal Engineer commented that unimplemented  20%  DF  projects
in CY 2016 were all completed in CY 2017.

      Also, the Municipal Vice Mayor asserted that job orders  salaries  and
wages were charged against the  20%  DF  since  they  were  responsible  for
maintaining the environment by planting  trees  and  taking  care  of  them.
However, they are willing to comply with our  recommendation  and  committed
to provide appropriation for the   salaries and wages of the job  orders  in
the general fund proper.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to strictly enforce  the  provision
of the Department of Social Welfare and Development Memorandum Circular  No.
04 series of 2015 and LGU Implementing Guidelines relative to the  grant  of
financial assistance. Submit the required supporting documents to  ascertain
that  said  recipients  are  qualified  as  beneficiaries  of  the  program,
otherwise the claims shall not be allowed in audit.

      The Municipal Social Welfare  Development  Officer  (MSWDO)  submitted
documents relative to claim of Shelter Assistance.

We recommended that the Municipality  refrain  from  the  practice  of
purchasing  gasoline  through  reimbursement  and  adopt   the   law   on
procurement which clearly provides in Republic Act No. 9184   to  promote
transparency in the mode of procurement applied by the municipality.

Management’s Comment

      During the exit  conference,  the  Municipal  Treasurer  informed  the
Audit Team that the recommendation will be implemented in the coming year.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  (LCE)  coordinate  the
efforts  of  public  and  private  sectors  and  implement  the  five  basic
environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment  and  public
health as required under R.A. No. 9003.  We  also  recommend  that  the  LCE
encourage the full participation of the barangays in the  implementation  of
the law.

Management’s Comment

      During the exit conference, the Municipal Vice Mayor commented that
since the Municipality is a protected area maintaining dumpsite might
affect the aquatic resources in the Taal lake. He also added that various
households maintained compost pit wherein the garbage and waste  were
thrown.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:


     a. Municipal Budget Officer  to  present  in  the  Annual  Budget  the
        breakdown of the  Mitigation Fund  as to the  amount   appropriated
        to Capital Outlay and  Maintenance  and  Other  Operating  Expenses
        (MOOE)  in order to easily determine the unexpended balance  to  be
        transferred to Trust Fund;


     b. Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management  Officer  (LDRRMO)  to
        prepare and submit  to  the  local  sanggunian  through  the  Local
        Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and   Management   Council   and   Local
        Development Council the annual LDRRMO Plan and budget specifying in
        detail the list of programs, activities and projects which  include
        under a separate caption those projects and programs charged to the
        unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, and implement at the  intended
        period of its implementation to ensure that the purpose  for  which
        the fund was established are met;


     c. Municipal Accountant to disclose in  the  Notes  to  the  Financial
        Statements the amount and details  of  the  unexpended  balance  of
        LDRRMF in order to inform the users  of  relevant  information  and
        fair presentation of financial statements; and


     d. LDRRMO to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF  on
        or before the 15th day after the end of each month  through  LDRRMC
        and Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA Auditor pursuant  to
        COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal  Accountant
to regularly prepare and submit the required  reports  and  strictly  adhere
with  the  rules  on  disclosing  information  in  the  Notes  to  Financial
Statements.

Management’s Comment

      During the exit conference, the  Municipal  Accountant   reasoned  out
that preparation of the required reports could not be made possible  due  to
manpower constraint   and   difficulty  in   the   determining  the  correct
presentation of these reports.

Auditor’s Rejoinder

      The Audit Team maintains its stand that these reports be prepared  and
submitted since it formed part of the municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Vice-Mayor, Sangguniang Bayan
Members and the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the  provisions
of RA 7160 and  National  Budget  Circular  No.  563  in  the  appropriation
relative to  payment  to  various  leagues  and  ensures  that  payments  of
membership fees and  annual  dues  to private organization  shall  not be
charged against the government funds.

      We also recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant  to  require  the  elected  officials  to
submit the prescribed official receipt of the Republic  of  the  Philippines
in relation to the payment of their  annual  dues,  membership  fees  and
other fees paid to their respective leagues considering that they  are
government organization.

Management’s Comment

      During the exit conference, the  Municipal  Budget  Officer  commented
that they no longer allowed the charging of annual dues and membership  fees
of various officials from the municipal fund.

      While, one Sangguniang Bayan member informed the Audit Team that  they
already coordinated with the President of the Philippine Councilor’s  League
Batangas Chapter about the prescribed official receipt.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned  Department
of  Education  officials  to  immediately  submit  the  above   mentioned
supporting documents to  substantiate  the transaction  and  the  Municipal
Accountant not to allow processing of claims that  are  not  supported  with
the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly comply with the requirements/procedures stated in Republic Act (RA) 9184. We also recommended that the Invitation to Bid (ITB), Notice to Award, Notice to Proceed and Approved Contracts be posted in PhilGEPS and conspicuous places to draw more interested/prospective bidders that would end result to competitive bidding to attain top quality and reasonable price to the advantage/benefit of the Municipality as well as promote transparency and full disclosure.
observations.managementAction: Posting of Invitation to Bid, Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed was not regularly done by the BAC members.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan to facilitate the passing of the resolution authorizing the former to apply for electric connection with BATELEC to commence the operation of the waterworks system.

We advised the concerned municipal officials to properly plan the execution for all the projects to be implemented by the municipality to avoid delay or similar case in the future.
observations.managementAction:
Sangguniang Bayan Resolution was passed authorizing the Municipal Mayor to apply for electrical connection with BATELEC.   The waterworks system had already an electrical connection, however, the pipeline that will connect to  every concessionaire was not yet available.

Another waterworks system was installed in Barangay Balete but it is still unoperational at year end due to the same problem last year.
observations.reason:
Lack of fund to implement the full operation of the waterworks system.
No available fund to carry out the possible application for electrical connection with BATELEC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other municipal employees to coordinate with the Department of Interior and Local Government and the AO25 Technical Working Group for the guidelines/criteria as basis in the granting of PBB for CY 2015.  Submit the necessary documents to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management failed to submit the required documents hence, the Notice of Suspension issued by the Audit Team matured into disallowance.  Some municipal officials and employees were already refunded the   PBB in installments.  While, those who retired from the service,  it was deducted from their terminal leave claims.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

Municipal Accountant to process claims on monetization of leave credits based on Civil Service Commission Omnibus Rules on Leave, Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order (EO) No. 292 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2002-001; 

Head of the Personnel Office to adequately supervise and review the work of the respective employee who is in charge of the leave cards to ensure the correctness and accuracy of the leave credits balances of municipal employees and compliance with prescribed Civil Service Law and Rules; and

Concerned employees to ensure that the required number of days to be monetized must be within Section 22, Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO 292.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant see to it that monetization of leave credits was based on CSC Omnibus Rules on Leave.

The Personnel Officer coordinated with the Civil Service and made clarification on the computation of leave credits.

Monetization of leave in excess of ten days was supported with reason/justification as prescribed by the CSC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management establish clear cut guidelines on the reimbursement of cell card expenses, duly supported with certification of the concerned personnel that this was used for the discharge of their official duties and functions. Set minimum limit on mobile expenses taking into account the nature of the work and the necessity on the use of the mobile phones. See to it that all calls in the landline are official in nature, duly certified by the Local Chief Executive.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan approved guidelines on the reimbursement of cellcards.
observations.reason: No reason given by Management for the certification that all calls are official in nature.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that requisition and procurement of medicines be supported with a certification from the Municipal Health Officer that the drugs and medicines being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF as required in DOH Administrative Order No. 163 series of 2002. Ensure that only those drugs and medicines that conform and listed in the Essential Drugs List of the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) be requested and procured. Otherwise, prior request with written justification should be made to the Head of the National Drug Policy Staff or to National Formulary Committee for the exceptions, to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Health Officer  see to it  that  all  drugs  and medicines purchased by the Municipality conformed with the latest PNDF.  However, the required certification was not attached to the disbursement voucher.
observations.reason: No reason given by the Municipal Health Officer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the municipal official and employees immediately refund all cash advances which are not used and/or excess on cash advances to the collecting officer as required in Item 5.7 of COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid loss and misuse of government fund.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant regularly monitor the unliquidated cash advances by sending demand letters to the concerned officials/employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period as required in the same circular.
observations.managementAction:
Whenever there is an unliquidated cash advance of municipal officials and employees, the Municipal Accountant deducted the amount from the payroll immediately.

The Municipal Accountant regularly monitor the cash advances made by the municipal officials and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to return the PDAF balances to the National Treasury for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in the General Fund of the national government.
observations.managementAction: Remitted to National Treasury per Check No. 0000395168 dated June 13, 2016 in the amount of P115,281.64. While, the amount of P10,543.40 was remitted under Check No. 0000513471 dated June 13, 2016.
